visualGoal: Prove the three helicopter controls with real product photos: a precise center-sprung stick as the CYCLIC, a friction-holding throttle lever as the COLLECTIVE, and dedicated pedals for the ANTI-TORQUE axis. Make a fixed-wing simmer understand a yoke is useless here and that a ~$300-400 repurposed rig flies MSFS 2024 helicopters well. Real product proof only; no procedural art. The prior pass wrongly embedded an unverified Komodo collective photo — that is now text-only.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: File exists on disk at this path but is NOT in verified-product-images.json, so it was REMOVED as an embedded image and made text-only in this rewrite. Needs vision-verification against the real hand-built Komodo B206 collective before it can appear as a gearpick/shotgrid or before certification. Official product page (komodosimulations.co.uk/products/b206) currently throws a TLS cert-altname error; confirm a working source.

Named as the budget cyclic (~$79.99) but pictured via the verified TCA Sidestick. A space-bay thrustmaster-t16000m-fcs.jpg exists but is not in the flight verified set. Source/verify a flight-bay photo if we want the T.16000M shown as its own visual.
Budget anti-torque pedal entry (~$180). Image FILE exists on disk but is NOT in verified-product-images.json — text-only until vision-verified and added to the verified manifest.
Dedicated-tier pedals ($599.99). No local image at all. Text-only; source a real photo before any body visual.
HOTAS-combo tier ($249.99); twin throttle as collective. No local image. Text-only.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic home flight-sim desk at cool evening rim light showing the helicopter control triad laid out ready to fly: a center-sprung stick at right knee (cyclic), a throttle-quadrant lever to the left set low (collective), and rudder pedals on the floor (anti-torque), with open overlay space for a real product photo on each, curator Mac Donovan seated in a settled-hover posture, no fake branded hardware, no fake sim UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake collective, warped controls, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art, a yoke (helicopters have no yoke)

infographicPrompts.title: The three helicopter controls
content: COLLECTIVE = total lift/power (lever that stays parked)
ANTI-TORQUE PEDALS = yaw (constant input, never resting)
A yoke maps to NONE of these
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, large readable labels, flight-cluster colors, real product photos as row markers for Codex overlay (Sidestick / TCA Quadrant / Charlie pedals)

infographicPrompts.title: MSFS 2024 helicopter bindings that actually work
content: 2 SET CYCLIC LONGITUDINAL/LATERAL AXIS + -30% to -40% curve
4 Don't confuse SET HELICOPTER THROTTLE 1 AXIS with collective
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered checklist, in-sim binding labels in monospace, warning accent on step 4

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult flight-sim curator Mac Donovan at a tidy home cockpit, left hand resting low on a throttle-quadrant lever positioned as a collective, calm instructor energy, product overlay space beside the lever, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.description: MSFS 2024 Controls screen showing COLLECTIVE AXIS assigned under Power Management with the Reverse/invert checkbox ticked, and the separate SET HELICOPTER THROTTLE 1 AXIS visible — the exact screen the binding section describes. Capture at 1080p, crop to the relevant rows.
screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.notes: Would strongly support the bindings section and the forum callout; not required for certification. Must be a genuine in-sim capture, not a mockup.

openQuestions: Confirm exact current Komodo collective price (official page cert error) and source a verified photo before it can be a body visual.

Should the T.16000M FCS get a verified flight-bay photo, or is picturing it via the TCA Sidestick acceptable long-term?
